manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: marble, octopus, stadium, fish, cover, hospital, microscope, knight, berlin.
Your opponent's team words are: fly, crane, turkey, copper, line, square, cell, tooth.
Distractor words are: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
The kill words are: mammoth.

message.reasoning:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Provide a single-word clue and a list of target words from my team's list.
    *   Format: Strict format required (`CLUE: <WORD>`, `TARGETS: <TARGETS>`). No extra text.
    *   Goal: Connect as many of my team's words as possible without connecting opponent's, distractor, or kill words.
    *   My Team Words: marble, octopus, stadium, fish, cover, hospital, microscope, knight, berlin.
    *   Opponent Words: fly, crane, turkey, copper, line, square, cell, tooth.
    *   Distractor Words: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
    *   Kill Word: mammoth.

2.  **Analyze My Team Words:**

    *   octopus (animal, sea creature)
    *   stadium (building, sports)
    *   fish (animal, sea creature)
    *   cover (verb, noun, insurance?)
    *   hospital (building, health)
    *   microscope (tool, science, small things)
    *   knight (chess, medieval, person)
    *   berlin (city, capital)

3.  **Identify Potential Connections:**
    *   *Sea/Water:* octopus, fish. (marble? no. stadium? no.)
    *   *Buildings/Places:* stadium, hospital, berlin (city). (marble? no.)
    *   *Chess:* knight. (king? queen? rook? bishop? pawn?) None other match.
    *   *Germany/Europe:* berlin. (marble? no.)

    *   *Protection:* cover, knight (protects king?), hospital (health cover?).
    *   *Materials:* marble, copper (opp), tooth (opp).
    *   *Animals:* octopus, fish, turkey (opp), crane (opp), fly (opp), mammoth (kill).

        *   *Glass:* microscope (lens), marble (glass toy?), stadium (glass windows?), hospital (glass?).

        *   *Tank:* fish (tank), octopus (tank?), stadium (tank?), knight (tank?).
        *   *Board:* chess (knight), game (marble?), stadium (board?).
        *   *Blue:* fish (blue fish?), berlin (blue?), marble (blue?).
        *   *Rock:* marble (rock), stadium (rock concert?), knight (hard as rock?).
        *   *Cell:* hospital (cell?), microscope (cell), opp word is 'cell'. Risky.
        *   *Scale:* fish (scales), stadium (scale?), marble (scale?), microscope (scale?).
        *   *Slide:* microscope (slide), stadium (slide?), cover (slide?).
        *   *Lens:* microscope, camera?, eye?
        *   *Pet:* fish, octopus (?), turkey (opp).
        *   *Sea:* octopus, fish.
        *   *Capital:* berlin, state (distractor), square (opp?).

        *   *Chess:* knight.
        *   *Toy:* marble, knight (chess piece), fish (toy?), octopus (toy?).

        *   *Small:* marble, microscope (looks at small), fish (some are small), octopus (some are small).
        *   *Specimen:* microscope, fish, octopus, hospital (patient?).
        *   *View:* microscope, stadium (view), cover (view?).
        *   *Operation:* hospital, knight (operation chess?), cover (operation?).
        *   *Check:* knight (checkers/chess), hospital (check-up), cover (check?).
        *   *Board:* knight (chess board), game (marble), stadium (scoreboard?).
        *   *Piece:* knight (chess piece), marble (piece?), fish (piece?).
        *   *Glass:* microscope, marble (shooter), hospital (windows).
        *   *Tank:* fish tank, octopus tank, stadium (tank?), knight (tank?).
        *   *Blue:* Berlin (blues?), fish (blue), marble (blue).
        *   *Wall:* Berlin Wall, Stadium Wall, Hospital Wall, Cover (wall-to-wall?).
        *   *Guard:* Knight (guards), Hospital (guards?), Cover (guards).
        *   *Care:* Hospital, Cover (insurance care), Fish (care?), Octopus (care?).
        *   *Unit:* Hospital (ward/unit), Stadium (unit?), Berlin (unit?).

    *   *Potential Group 3: Chess/Game.*

    *   Opponent Words: fly, crane, turkey, copper, line, square, cell, tooth.

    *   Kill Word: mammoth.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: marble, octopus, stadium, fish, cover, hospital, microscope, knight, berlin.
Your opponent's team words are: fly, crane, turkey, copper, line, square, cell, tooth.
Distractor words are: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
The kill words are: mammoth.

CLUE: TANK
TARGETS: fish, octopus

The word fish was guessed correctly. The word copper was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: marble, octopus, stadium, cover, hospital, microscope, knight, berlin.
Remaining words for your opponent are: fly, crane, turkey, line, square, cell, tooth.
Remaining distractor words are: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
Remaining kill words are: mammoth.

CLUE: SHIELD
TARGETS: knight, cover

The words knight, cover were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: marble, octopus, stadium, hospital, microscope, berlin.
Remaining words for your opponent are: fly, crane, line, square, cell, tooth.
Remaining distractor words are: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
Remaining kill words are: mammoth.

CLUE: GLASS
TARGETS: microscope, marble

The words microscope, marble were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: octopus, stadium, hospital, berlin.
Remaining words for your opponent are: fly, crane, line, cell, tooth.
Remaining distractor words are: england, bell, degree, foot, wake, bill, state.
Remaining kill words are: mammoth.

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
